title: Explainer on the Interconnection Final Rule (FERC Order No. 2023)
claim_supported: FERC Order No. 2023 (issued July 28, 2023; published in Federal Register Sept 6, 2023) replaces first-come-first-served serial interconnection studies with a 'first ready, first served' cluster-study process, adds financial-deposit/site-control requirements, firm study deadlines with penalties, and a new affected-systems process; Order No. 2023-A (rehearing order) issued March 21, 2024 with compliance-filing deadline April 3, 2024.
locator: FERC explainer page (direct fetch returned HTTP 403 this session; details reconstructed from WebSearch synthesis of FERC.gov and law-firm summaries -- e.g. K&L Gates, Troutman Pepper, Orrick)

claim_supported: IEC 61803:2020 (Edition 2.0, published Oct 19, 2020) standardizes procedures for determining total power losses of line-commutated HVDC converter stations (12-pulse thyristor converters primarily); updated from the 1999 first edition to allow manufacturer production records for loss calculation.

title: IEC TS 63102:2021 -- Grid code compliance assessment methods for grid connection of wind and PV power plants
claim_supported: IEC TS 63102:2021 specifies recommended technical methods (compliance testing, simulation, and monitoring) for grid-code compliance assessment of wind/PV plant grid connections, covering frequency/voltage range, reactive power capability, active/reactive control performance, fault ride-through, and power quality -- relevant baseline for grid-forming inverter-based-resource compliance frameworks.
locator: IEC Webstore publication page (found via search; not independently fetched this session)
notes: Not directly fetched this session; details from search-result synthesis of the IEC/en-standard.eu listing pages.

title: Commission Regulation (EU) 2016/1447 establishing a network code on requirements for grid connection of high voltage direct current systems and direct current-connected power park modules
claim_supported: EU Network Code on HVDC Connections (adopted 26 Aug 2016, entered into force 8 Sept 2016) sets binding technical requirements for HVDC systems and DC-connected power-park-module grid connections (frequency range, voltage control, fault ride-through, reactive power, protection); ACER published Recommendation 01/2024 (Dec 19, 2024) proposing amendments to expand scope to offshore demand facilities, electrolysers, offshore storage, and HVDC links to isolated AC networks.
locator: EUR-Lex official regulation text, fetched in full
notes: Geography 'EU' used as a supranational quasi-ISO code for a regulation applicable across all EU member states, not a single country.

title: IEEE 2800-2022 -- IEEE Standard for Interconnection and Interoperability of Inverter-Based Resources (IBRs) Interconnecting with Associated Transmission Electric Power Systems
claim_supported: IEEE 2800-2022 sets uniform minimum interconnection/performance requirements for inverter-based resources at >=69kV transmission/sub-transmission voltage (voltage/frequency ride-through, active/reactive power control, dynamic support, power quality, protection); explicitly applies to isolated IBRs connected via dedicated VSC-HVDC facilities; parts adopted into NERC PRC-029. Companion IEEE 2800.2-2026 (test/verification procedures) published spring 2026.
locator: IEEE Xplore standard document page (WebFetch returned no extractable content this session; details from WebSearch synthesis of IEEE Xplore/ANSI/PES listing pages)
notes: Direct WebFetch of the IEEE Xplore standard page returned an empty result this session; standard's existence, scope, and adoption by NERC are corroborated across multiple independent sources (IEEE-PES, ANSI webstore, MISO PAC filing).

title: Voluntary Specification for Grid-forming Inverters: Core Requirements Test Framework
claim_supported: AEMO's Voluntary Specification for Grid-forming Inverters (published Jan 17, 2024) sets a voluntary simulation-based test/verification framework -- performance-assessment protocols, grid-fault-interaction scenarios, technical parameters -- for pre-deployment validation of grid-forming inverters in Australia's National Electricity Market.
locator: AEMO PDF document, fetched in full (2.8MB)

authors_or_org: U.S. Department of Energy Grid Deployment Office; National Renewable Energy Laboratory (NREL); Pacific Northwest National Laboratory (PNNL)
claim_supported: DOE Grid Deployment Office, with NREL and PNNL, ran a multi-year (launched March 15 2022, published Oct 3 2024) National Transmission Planning Study analyzing 96 scenarios of contiguous-US transmission needs through 2050; per independent trade coverage (Utility Dive) cited during this search, an HVDC-centric buildout scenario shows the largest projected savings (~$490bn) versus AC (~$270bn) or point-to-point (~$380bn) buildouts by 2050 -- this comparative figure was not independently re-confirmed from the primary DOE report text this session and should be treated as provisional pending direct verification.
locator: DOE GDO study landing page, fetched in full; comparative HVDC/AC savings figures sourced from Utility Dive trade reporting found via WebSearch, not confirmed in the fetched DOE page itself
notes: The DOE landing page fetched this session did not itself contain the HVDC-vs-AC savings comparison; that figure is flagged as provisional/second-hand (Utility Dive) rather than directly sourced -- verifier must open the full NTPS report PDF to confirm before this becomes a load-bearing claim.

title: 系統連系技術要件 (Grid Interconnection Technical Requirements), effective April 2023
authors_or_org: Tokyo Electric Power Company Power Grid, Inc. (TEPCO Power Grid)
claim_supported: TEPCO Power Grid's mandatory grid-interconnection technical requirements document (effective April 1, 2023, per its filename/title), governing technical conditions for connecting generation/storage assets to the TEPCO service-territory grid.

As a regulated monopoly grid operator publishing mandatory interconnection rules, TEPCO PG functions in a quasi-regulator capacity for this document -- flagged for verifier to confirm DC/HVDC-specific content, which was not readable this session.

title: SCR-based Medium Voltage DC Solid-State Circuit Breaker
claim_supported: ORNL researchers built and tested a 1.5kV/300A SCR-based solid-state DC circuit-breaker prototype for MVDC applications, demonstrating interruption of a 200A load current in under 50 microseconds.
locator: ORNL IMPACT publications repository record (found via search; not independently fetched this session)
notes: Classified as national_lab rather than academic_peer_reviewed since this is ORNL's own institutional repository record; verifier should check whether the underlying work was also published in a peer-reviewed journal/conference for a possible dual/upgraded classification.

claim_supported: Hitachi Energy markets a commercial Hybrid HVDC Breaker as part of its HVDC portfolio, evolving from ABB's original hybrid-breaker demonstration; page fetched did not contain specific voltage/current/interruption-time ratings or pricing.

claim_supported: Prysmian's 525kV HVDC cable systems (XLPE, P-Laser, and MIND technologies) support submarine bipole capacity up to 2.5GW, nearly double comparable 320kV DC systems; conductor cross-sections range 1200-3500mm2 depending on technology/conductor material; no pricing information published.
